keyword: what does a podcast production agency do
title_suggestion: What Does Done-For-You Podcast Production Actually Include?
brief: Explain exactly what APG delivers per episode: video podcast, audio podcast, guest intro trailer, 6 short-form clips, 3 thumbnails, show notes, SEO article, distribution. Cover the pre-production briefing process (both host and guest receive briefs). Mention 10 business day turnaround. Include a section on what clients DON'T have to do. Reference APG's Irish Podcast Award wins. Embed a guest intro trailer Wistia video (ID: ypq7ryjsr8) as an example. Target keyword: 'what does a podcast production agency do'. Secondary: 'done for you podcast production', 'full service podcast production'. Word count: 1800-2500.
data_points: Award-winning production recognised at Irish Podcast Awards
10 business day turnaround from order onboarding
Every episode includes video, audio, trailer, 6 clips, 3 thumbnails, show notes, SEO article

title_suggestion: How We Use Guest Recruitment to Put Your Ideal Clients in Front of Your Microphone
brief: Explain APG's guest recruitment process: ICP definition, research, qualification, outreach, follow-ups, scheduling. Frame guest recruitment as a sales strategy, not just content. Explain the Pipeline Podcast methodology: guests become prospects, prospects become partners, partners become revenue. Use the 10% guest-to-client conversion stat. Cover how APG handles all communication on behalf of the client. This is APG's biggest differentiator - no competitor covers this well. Target keyword: 'podcast guest recruitment strategy'. Secondary: 'how to find podcast guests B2B', 'podcast guest as lead generation'. Word count: 2000-2500.
data_points: 10% of podcast guests convert to customers or long-term partnerships
APG handles all outreach, follow-ups, and scheduling
Every guest is qualified against the client's ideal customer profile

keyword: how much does B2B podcast production cost
title_suggestion: How Much Does B2B Podcast Production Cost in 2026? An Honest Breakdown
brief: Be transparent about podcast production costs. Break down the industry: DIY ($0-200/mo), freelance editor ($300-800/mo), production agency ($1,500-5,000+/mo), full done-for-you with strategy ($2,000-7,000+/mo). Explain what you get at each level. Position APG's Brand Builder as the premium done-for-you option. Discuss why cheap production is a false economy for B2B. Cover hidden costs of DIY (time, opportunity cost, inconsistency). This is high-intent content - people searching this are comparing providers. Target keyword: 'how much does podcast production cost'. Secondary: 'podcast production agency pricing', 'done for you podcast cost'. Word count: 2000-2500.
data_points: APG offers two fixed tiers: production-only and full done-for-you including guest recruitment
6-month minimum commitment
Consistent monthly production compounds results over time

title_suggestion: Podcast ROI for B2B: Why We Measure Leads, Not Downloads
brief: Challenge the industry obsession with download numbers. Argue that for B2B, podcast ROI should be measured in: guest relationships converting to deals, referrals from guests, authority positioning leading to inbound leads, strategic ad revenue, and content assets generated per episode. Use APG's 10% conversion stat. Explain why a B2B podcast with 200 listeners can be more valuable than a consumer podcast with 10,000 if those 200 are decision makers. Cover the compounding effect of consistent monthly production. Target keyword: 'podcast ROI for B2B'. Secondary: 'is podcasting worth it for B2B', 'podcast lead generation B2B'. Word count: 2000-2500.
data_points: 10% guest-to-client conversion rate
Quality of audience matters more than quantity for B2B
Consistent monthly production compounds pipeline over time

Discuss the growth of podcasting in Ireland and the EU market opportunity for B2B founders. keyword: should my company start a podcast
title_suggestion: Should Your B2B Company Start a Podcast? A Decision Framework
brief: Provide an honest decision framework. Cover when a podcast IS right: you have a clear ICP, you want to build relationships with prospects, you can commit to consistency, you have something valuable to say. Cover when it is NOT right: you want quick results, you are not willing to show up consistently, your audience does not consume audio/video content. Discuss the minimum viable commitment (6 months). Position launching with an agency vs DIY. Be direct and opinionated - this builds trust. Target keyword: 'should my company start a podcast'. Secondary: 'is a podcast worth it for B2B founders', 'B2B podcast best practices'. Word count: 1800-2200.
data_points: 6-month minimum commitment reflects the time needed to see compounding results
Podcasting is a long-term strategy with short-term relationship wins built in

title_suggestion: 10% of Our Clients' Podcast Guests Become Customers. Here Is How.
brief: Deep dive into APG's signature stat. Explain the mechanics: pre-qualified guests who match the ICP, pre-production briefing creates a focused valuable conversation, the recording builds genuine rapport, post-episode follow-up nurtures the relationship, guest receives high-quality assets they want to share. data_points: 10% average guest-to-client/partner conversion rate

keyword: video podcast vs audio podcast B2B

title_suggestion: Podcasting for High-Trust Industries: Why Legal, Financial, and Consulting Firms Are Launching Shows

Reference APG's experience working with law firms and B2B founders in high-trust sectors. Target keyword: 'podcast for law firms'. title_suggestion: Hire a Podcast Agency or Do It Yourself? What B2B Founders Need to Know
brief: Honest comparison of DIY vs agency podcast production. Cover time investment (DIY takes 15-25 hours per episode when you include editing, show notes, clips, distribution vs 1-2 hours with an agency). Cover quality difference. Cover opportunity cost for founders. Cover when DIY makes sense (you enjoy it, budget is tight, you want to learn). Cover when an agency makes sense (you value time, you want consistent quality, you want guest recruitment handled). Be fair but make the case for why founders' time is better spent on conversations, not editing. Target keyword: 'in-house vs outsourced podcast production'. Secondary: 'podcast agency vs freelancer', 'should I hire a podcast producer'. Word count: 2000-2500.
data_points: APG handles everything so founders only spend time on the conversation itself
10 business day turnaround
Full deliverable set per episode saves 15-20 hours of DIY work

title_suggestion: How to Monetise Your B2B Podcast Without a Single Sponsor
brief: Challenge the assumption that podcast monetisation means sponsorships. For B2B, the real monetisation is: converting guests to clients (10% stat), strategic ad placements for your own services, building authority that drives inbound leads, creating a content engine that reduces marketing spend, and generating referrals. Cover APG's approach to building monetisation into every episode from day one. Discuss the difference between B2C monetisation (ads, Patreon) and B2B monetisation (pipeline, deals, partnerships). Target keyword: 'how to monetise a B2B podcast'. Secondary: 'podcast monetisation strategy', 'podcast revenue without sponsors'. Word count: 1800-2200.
data_points: APG builds strategic ad placements into every episode
10% guest-to-client conversion rate
Monetisation is structured from day one, not an afterthought
